commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto you pure beaten olive oil for the light, to cause the lamps to burn continually. It is probable that this order was given while Beseleel was working at the tabernacle. (Calmet) The people were to furnish the necessary sacrifices, by the half sicle, Exodus xxx. 13, and by voluntary contributions on the three great festivals, on which no one was to appear empty-handed, Exodus xxiii. Some chose to put their contributions towards the temple in the treasury, Luke xxi. Oil: Hebrew, "pure oil of the olive beaten, for light to".
